summary: Large mnmainrule decompilation PR replacing multiple placeholder stubs with C implementations and expanding the menu rule data structures/prototypes needed to type those functions. The automated decomp.dev report showed 2 new full matches, including mn_80230198 and mn_802307F8, plus sizable improvements for fn_8022F538, mn_80230274, fn_802309F0, mn_8022FEC8, mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, and mn_80230E38. The grhomerun changes appear to be formatting-only cleanup and not central to the PR.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8022F538, the main rules menu input handler, replacing a decomp placeholder with logic for button handling, hovered/confirmed selection changes, entering item switch/special menu paths, backing out, writing GameRules fields, and playing menu audio.

Implemented helper/update functions mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, mn_8022FEC8, mn_80230198, mn_80230274, mn_802307F8, fn_802309F0, and mn_80230E38, with several still reported as partial improvements rather than full matches by decomp.dev.
Expanded mnmainrule.h's struct mn_802307F8_t from broad padding into named byte fields x3, x4, x5, x6, x9, xA, a union exposing HSD_JObj* xC[17], xC0[10], and x34[7], and the HSD_Text* text at 0x130.

Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes for mnmainrule functions with typed signatures such as void mn_8022FB88(u8, void*), void mn_8022FD18(u8), void mn_8022FEC8(HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, u8, u8), void mn_80230198(HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, u8), void mn_80230274(HSD_GObj*, int, int), and void fn_802309F0(HSD_GObj*).
Added local temporary structs in mnmainrule.c for inferred layout access, including mn_803EC818_t, mn_8022FB88_arg1_t, and mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t.
Added data definitions for AnimLoopSettings arrays mn_803EC734[5] and mn_803EC770[9], plus many externs for menu constants/globals used by the new implementations.
Updated mn_80230D18 to use struct mn_802307F8_t directly instead of the removed local mn_80230D18_t, preserving initialization of menu state and JObj slots while using the refined field names.

Used JObj creation/loading, animation requests, flag toggling, and child attachment for menu cursor/options through HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_JObjReqAnim/ReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnim/AnimAll, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, and HSD_JObjAddChild.
Updated text creation in mn_802307F8 to use extern float globals mn_804DBE18 through mn_804DBE2C instead of hardcoded constants in the prior partial implementation.
Adjusted grhomerun formatting around long casts/calls/expressions without apparent behavioral changes.
smart_moves: Promoted opaque header prototypes to typed signatures as functions were decompiled, reducing UNK_PARAMS propagation and making later callers easier to match.
Kept partially-understood layouts honest by using offset-style field names such as x0, x1, x2, x9, xA, xC, and x34 rather than inventing semantic names not supported by the diff.

Used unions/overlayed structs in mn_802307F8_t to express two observed views of the same JObj region: a flat xC[17] array and grouped xC0/x34 access, which helps match pointer arithmetic while documenting layout.
Extracted animation frame ranges into AnimLoopSettings arrays and reused existing helpers mn_8022ED6C and mn_8022F298 for frame-loop behavior instead of open-coding all animation state checks.
Preserved byte-packing behavior with stack locals and casts such as u8* over u16/s32 values for packed JObj indices, a common tactic when matching code that iterates over bytes of constants.

Used existing game/menu globals and helper functions instead of introducing abstractions, which is often important for matching: mn_804A04F0, gmMainLib_8015CC34, gm_801A4310, mn_803EC600, mn_803EC7DC, and mn_803EB6B0.
Matched special menu behavior for gm_801A4310() == 0x1B by skipping option index 4 in selection loops and handling time-limit constraints, preserving mode-specific branch shape.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, a useful first step is to type the per-menu user_data struct enough to replace byte offsets with xN fields while retaining exact offsets in comments.
Do not over-name menu rule fields until semantics are clear; offset-derived names remain searchable and prevent misleading future work.

JObj-heavy UI functions often need arrays of child roots gathered by lb_8001204C/lb_80011E24 and then indexed by packed byte constants; modeling those packed indices can unlock otherwise awkward loops.

Existing sdata/sdata2 floats may need to be declared as externs and used directly for matching instead of replacing them with literal constants, as seen in mn_802307F8 text placement/font-size updates.

GameRules synchronization appears repeatedly after confirmed menu changes; future work should look for repeated assignments to mode, time_limit, handicap, damage_ratio, unk_x7, and stock_count as a matching pattern.
Partial matches can still be valuable when they expose struct layout and call graph shape; decomp.dev reported large byte improvements even where functions remained below 100%.
When a mode hides a menu option, preserve the original skip logic in both traversal and visible-index calculation; here the 0x1B mode skipped option 4 in several loops.
Small unrelated formatting changes can be present in decomp PRs; separate them from the substantive matching record unless they affect codegen.
naming_conventions: Unidentified menu functions kept address-based names such as fn_8022F538, mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, mn_8022FEC8, mn_80230198, mn_80230274, fn_802309F0, mn_80230D18, and mn_80230E38.
Inferred struct fields used offset-style names, for example x0, x1, x2, x3, x4, x5, x6, x9, xA, xC, x34, text.
Temporary inferred types used function/address-scoped names such as mn_8022FB88_arg1_t and mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t.
Globals retained project address-style names such as mn_804D6BD0, mn_804A04F0, mn_803EC600, mn_803EC734, mn_803EC770, mn_803EC7DC, and mn_804DBE18.
Header prototypes include address comments like /* 22FB88 */ and use concrete types once known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198, indicating explicit stack-shape control for matching.
Used byte-wise pointer iteration over stack copies of packed constants/globals, for example casting addresses of u16/s32 locals to u8* before indexing.
Used casts into mn_803EC600 at fixed offsets to recover float animation settings and tables without fully naming the underlying data blob.
Used goto labels and intentionally direct control flow in mn_80230198 to reproduce compiler branch structure.
Used repeated gm_801A4310() checks and explicit comparisons rather than abstracting mode logic, preserving likely emitted branch patterns.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT after HSD_MemAlloc in mn_80230E38 for user_data allocation, preserving runtime assertion behavior and likely string reference.
Used direct writes through casted button storage, e.g. ((u32*) &mn_804A04F0.buttons)[1] and [0], to match the original data layout.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only issue comment was the automated decomp.dev report: matched code increased to 66.33% (+0.01%, +468 bytes), with 2 new matches and 10 improvements in unmatched items.
decomp.dev reported full matches for mn_80230198 (+220 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%) and mn_802307F8 (+35 bytes, 85.89% to 100.00%).

decomp.dev reported large partial improvements for mn_80230E38 (+1415 bytes to 69.26%), fn_8022F538 (+1357 bytes to 84.02%), mn_80230274 (+1010 bytes to 71.56%), fn_802309F0 (+759 bytes to 93.99%), mn_8022FEC8 (+587 bytes to 81.66%), mn_8022FB88 (+398 bytes to 99.50%), and mn_8022FD18 (+381 bytes to 88.20%).
decomp.dev also reported a small .sdata2 regression (-10 bytes, 50.00% to 36.36%).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching fn_8022F538, mn_80230274, fn_802309F0, mn_8022FEC8, mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, or mn_80230E38.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic field names for struct mn_802307F8_t fields x2/x3/x4/x5/x6/x9/xA.
Look for PRs that replace mn_803EC600 offset casts with a typed data/table struct.
Investigate whether the .sdata2 regression reported by decomp.dev was addressed in a follow-up PR.
Search for other menu-rule decomp PRs that use packed byte index iteration over u16/s32 globals for JObj arrays.
